payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, we had some cool Critical Role representation out on the Playas. That was really neat. Thank you so much. It was awesome to meet you, Scott, and thanks for coming by. Other fun announcements... This is a cool thing that came up, actually. Hasbro's working with Indiegogo right now to do essentially the search for their new face-to-face party game circumstance. So they're accepting a bunch of submissions right now. I think it's nextgreatgamechallenge.com, but if you're like me and spent a lot of your youth spending your summers designing your own card games and your own board games and your own party games and such that just sat there and did nothing, now is actually a really cool opportunity to submit those and possibly get them published. Because the person who wins, wins $10,000 on top of the fact that they get flown out to Hasbro, to the actual company building to work on developing the game with their development team. So that's pretty cool. (laughs) So those of you who have that little game design brain, go ahead and throw your ideas out that way. Submit to this! Hopefully you could win. The thing goes to September 30th? You don't have a whole lot of time, so get on it. But check it out! A possible opportunity for you to design your own game. Who knows, maybe one day we'll be playing it on this Twitch stream. So check that out. Also, Welovefine is running a t-shirt design contest for the upcoming Rage of Dragons Dungeons and Dragons release, and they've asked me to be one of the judges of that contest. So all you people with an artistic bone in your body, go ahead and design some t-shirts. Go to welovefine.com, check out the contest rules for the Rage of Demons contest. But I'll be judging your designs! And hopefully if yours is appreciated by me and all the other judges, you'll be getting a cash prize and have your shirts officially sanctioned by D&D and put on welovefine.com, so check that out! That's a cool thing! I've already seen some great art. Some people have floated over to Twitter. If I haven't responded, it's because I'm a judge and I probably shouldn't. (laughs) But everything looks cool, so thank you guys for your submissions.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that was a surrealist experience.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Oh, man. That was a lot of dry heaving for like twenty minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where's Orion?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Orion will be here in a second. He's missing his moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But Travis plays the lead villain!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Travis is the main villain! Scrotus!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes, Scabrous Scrotus. Who is in no way related to Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. He is large and has many curious accoutrements on his outfit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes he does! He has a giant horn on his crotch. Don't know what that's about.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Are we talking about Mad Max?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, we are.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hey, he's Scrotus!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes! And you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I play Scab and a bunch of war boys. So check it out! Because I work with this guy here!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And I'm a war boy commander and a bunch of other guys, so you hear a lot of me going, "Get that guy!" And a lot of random NPCs giving quests.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I think you hear me barfing a lot during that game.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah. So we will have a Liam possibly joining us later in the evening. He had some family commitments tonight, but he's going to hope to try and join us around 8:30 or 9:00, if at all.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Awesome. For the four of you that are interested in football watching the stream--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We all come from different walks, all right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know. No, that's awesome. Cool! There we go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Teach me of your ways.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) My sorcerer's ways.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) I was too much of an indoor kid. But I'm learning!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: There goes ball. Chase ball.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're growing! We're learning and growing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I am. I got really into rugby when we were in New Zealand. Oh, sports I can watch!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Rugby's dope, man. They're hardcore.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, they hit each other really hard. They call things a scrum. That sounds like fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That's rugby. Scrum.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's intense, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's some brutal stuff.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Such children. Responsible for the development and the progression of the guild itself, they found themselves having Pike join them for about a week's time while the landscape of her temple to Sarenrae was being cleansed of a curse, and the group decided to follow through on their promise to aid Keyleth on her Aramente to find the Pyrah people to the far southwest of Vasselheim.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If it's something you want to donate to, it's a cool project, you get to watch me be all evil and cast--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I watched it. It's really fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) You can see me cast necromantic spells at Kevin Sorbo. I mean, what more could you want, really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Our hair has a battle. It's wonderful.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Our mighty DM versus Hercules.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Indeed. I remember the first time we met on set, he came up to me, and goes like, "What's your mother's name?" I still wonder. But anyway. Let us bring us back to the story. Now, Vox Machina, after completing the trials of the Slayer's Take and acquiring their marking brand as essence of them joining the actual guild itself, meeting the gynosphinx that is currently responsible--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (chuckles)

payload.raw_text: Ugh. Garlic. Stinking Cloud over there. You thought he wasn't a caster. They made their way to the mountains, eventually to the actual Pyrah tribe itself, where they underwent a trial through the elemental plane of fire. Battled some of the druids, the Pyrah themselves, in elemental fire form, combated against time through a cinder storm as they attempted to escape, and managed to do so with their lives, and in doing so gained the blessing of the Aramente for Keyleth from the Pyrah tribe, as well as a blessing from the main druid as well, as a spark stone. And returned to Vasselheim, Pike then said her goodbyes again, returning to help the restoration of the temple of Sarenrae, and you've all been left to your devices now. So. As you all awaken the next morning within the guildhall itself, your journey and your path is your own to take.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Indeed. I remember the first time we met on set, he came up to me, and goes like, "What's your mother's name?" I still wonder. But anyway. Let us bring us back to the story. Now, Vox Machina, after completing the trials of the Slayer's Take and acquiring their marking brand as essence of them joining the actual guild itself, meeting the gynosphinx that is currently responsible--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Such children. Responsible for the development and the progression of the guild itself, they found themselves having Pike join them for about a week's time while the landscape of her temple to Sarenrae was being cleansed of a curse, and the group decided to follow through on their promise to aid Keyleth on her Aramente to find the Pyrah people to the far southwest of Vasselheim.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Ugh. Garlic. Stinking Cloud over there. You thought he wasn't a caster. They made their way to the mountains, eventually to the actual Pyrah tribe itself, where they underwent a trial through the elemental plane of fire. Battled some of the druids, the Pyrah themselves, in elemental fire form, combated against time through a cinder storm as they attempted to escape, and managed to do so with their lives, and in doing so gained the blessing of the Aramente for Keyleth from the Pyrah tribe, as well as a blessing from the main druid as well, as a spark stone. And returned to Vasselheim, Pike then said her goodbyes again, returning to help the restoration of the temple of Sarenrae, and you've all been left to your devices now. So. As you all awaken the next morning within the guildhall itself, your journey and your path is your own to take.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Indeed. I remember the first time we met on set, he came up to me, and goes like, "What's your mother's name?" I still wonder. But anyway. Let us bring us back to the story. Now, Vox Machina, after completing the trials of the Slayer's Take and acquiring their marking brand as essence of them joining the actual guild itself, meeting the gynosphinx that is currently responsible--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (chuckles)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Such children. Responsible for the development and the progression of the guild itself, they found themselves having Pike join them for about a week's time while the landscape of her temple to Sarenrae was being cleansed of a curse, and the group decided to follow through on their promise to aid Keyleth on her Aramente to find the Pyrah people to the far southwest of Vasselheim.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ugh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm burping garlic.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The travel back to the town took about two and a half, three days, so that's been long past the feast. So the benefits of the Heroes' Feast have already been washed from your system.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Beard check. (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Zero. Or ten?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's zero.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It depends.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's a hundred.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are you serious?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's the second time that's happened.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All you had to have rolled was a nine or anything other than a zero. That's all you had to-- anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If this had been the second edition Encyclopedia Magica, you would've gotten the most broken artifact ever. You rolled two hundreds. With a tiny methodical squeak, your pores produce but a few random molecules of hair-like material that then puff in the air around you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it at least rough? Like, you know, he needs to shave, it's so rough.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Nah, it's smooth as a baby's bottom.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well-oiled.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Fine leather. That's what your face feels like.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: It's still 24 hours since we had the feast, right? It still counts as the same time?

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to the main
payload.raw_text: As you make your way to the main thoroughfare, you look over and you catch the image of the distant goliath heading northward towards the Braving Grounds, just plowing through the crowd. You see a couple people that are knocked flat onto their ass, like head shaking, "what just happened?" as he just goes bounding into the distance.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Was there a Looney Tunes hole in the wall? (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: So as Grog bounds off into the distance, do you guys give chase?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You get out into the main thoroughfare.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: We really need to make that leash for him, like we said we were going to.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, the ones that you're keeping an eye on were from the Quad Roads, and they're still staying back. One of them, the one that you've marked, is around the corner, just keeping an eye on everybody, but he's mainly keeping to himself. He does stand out like a sore thumb because his armor is darker and based around Erathis, whereas here the Bastions that are under Kord's vision have more of a red, dark, almost rusty look to their armor. Anyway, as the shouts continue, some of the Kord Bastions gather, one of which you recognize as the one who was acting as the bookie the first time you came to the Crucible. And he looks-- wiping sleep from his eyes-- he looks like he just got into his armor and rushed out here for about ten minutes since you just came out here and shouted your challenge to the sky. He rushes out for a second. "You! Hey, am I to hear properly that you are challenging our standing champion once again?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He cocks his head a little bit at you. "Just a moment. Stay right there." And he runs back inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: There's going to be a street fight!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I hear Scanlan say that and I run out and I say, (shouting) fight!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, other members of the tavern are starting to come out, tankards in hand, like, "what's going on?" They see you. "Oh! There's going to be a fight at the Crucible!" You hear the shouts going off the nearby houses; it's echoing, like town crier to town crier. You're hearing voices echo throughout the entirety of the Braving Grounds: people preparing for this midday unexpected throwdown. At this point, you see a few of the Bastions wearing the armor that is geared towards Kord, the god of strength that runs this portion of Vasselheim.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The ones that I'm keeping an eye on?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (laughs) About 20 minutes pass by as the crowd is now gathered, about 50 to 60 people. Apparently, word of your last battle has gotten around, and now there's this fevered pitch of interest. People have dropped what they're doing and the smokestacks you can see have slowly turned to a dribble as everyone who's working the nearby forges and/or smelting irons have rushed out here to see this battle and dropped what they're doing. This has turned into full-on, "Morpheus is fighting Neo."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I join in with everybody else. Yeah, did you see that, Kern's fighting Philip!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Why are you telling me this? Of course I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Kern's fighting--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I also know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I try, subtly, to pass off my ring of AC to Grog?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Grog, do you have any enchantment of protection, currently?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do not?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: On the ring, does it say attunement required?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. It doesn't, I don't think. Hold on, let me look.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I can look it up for you, if need be.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ring of Protection.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, my Belt of Dwarvenkind is the only thing, but that's all.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It just says plus two bonus to AC and saving throw-- oh, requires attunement.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you have to take time to go ahead and attune to the magical item.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Damn it! I'm sorry, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You shouldn't have taken off running!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 1,363 gold pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I turn to the bookie, and I take out my entire coin purse, and I throw it at him.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I use my Mage Hand--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, don't use magic!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh no, I don't! I don't do that!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We whack him on the head before he starts to do the hand. Stop it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I throw him the coin purse and I say, I expect to see that doubled when I get out. And I jump into the pit before Kern even arrives.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You leap and the whole crowd goes (cheering) and this series of shouting screams surround the air around you. He grabs the coin purse-- how much is in that coin purse?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: According to this, it's eleven-- that can't be right, can it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Personal funds?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your personal funds, not what's in the Bag of Holding.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Coins start raining on you  copper, silver, and gold pieces spin down on the ground around you, Grog. You guys just stand tensely locked in that warrior glare. "Challenging him, once again, tail between his legs, intent on claiming his glory, returning to the Crucible, Philip."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No! Philip is dead. There is no Philip. There is only Grog, the Vengeful. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cheers go up around the outside of the Crucible. You can now see the entire crowd-- the energy itself is electric in the air; eyes are intense and bloodshot. Everyone is looking down into what is about to be, hopefully, a drag-out, throwdown fight. You feel the rippling strength of your stone giant elders tearing through your body. You flex, and you hear with each muscle tensing and the popping of bones and sinew as you prepare yourself for this. As you're doing this, you see across the way, Kern cracks his knuckles, and you can see the giant scar from his snarling lip. You realize he hasn't been snarling this whole time; it's just the natural state of his current smile. And then he snarls. You can see his orcish tusks protruding out, and the gums themselves, black and purple, peering out from underneath his upper lip that you once destroyed. "I'm going to take yours this time, boy."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Enough words, toothless. Let's do this.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I have a whole cluster over here. So what'd you get for initiative, in total?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Coins start raining on you  copper, silver, and gold pieces spin down on the ground around you, Grog. You guys just stand tensely locked in that warrior glare. "Challenging him, once again, tail between his legs, intent on claiming his glory, returning to the Crucible, Philip."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No! Philip is dead. There is no Philip. There is only Grog, the Vengeful. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cheers go up around the outside of the Crucible. You can now see the entire crowd-- the energy itself is electric in the air; eyes are intense and bloodshot. Everyone is looking down into what is about to be, hopefully, a drag-out, throwdown fight. You feel the rippling strength of your stone giant elders tearing through your body. You flex, and you hear with each muscle tensing and the popping of bones and sinew as you prepare yourself for this. As you're doing this, you see across the way, Kern cracks his knuckles, and you can see the giant scar from his snarling lip. You realize he hasn't been snarling this whole time; it's just the natural state of his current smile. And then he snarls. You can see his orcish tusks protruding out, and the gums themselves, black and purple, peering out from underneath his upper lip that you once destroyed. "I'm going to take yours this time, boy."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Enough words, toothless. Let's do this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I get advantage, don't I? That's better. 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Music change!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How did you have that ready?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Witchcraft!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I keep all my stuff at the ready, just in case. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Are we that predictable?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (whack, whack) Yeah. And for those who are paying attention: they're both raging, they both take half damage, but since they're both doing it, we're negating both abilities because otherwise this battle would take three hours. (whack, whack) These fists, you see as they hit you, they're not just extremely strong and powerful previously, but they're hitting precise areas where you feel the muscle and bone is less resistant. There is technique to these punches that wasn't there before.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's been training!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: He monked up!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And goes into swing two heavy fists at you. That is going to be a-- Let's see, 24?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, that is going to be a 27.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that'll hit, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, first hit is nine points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Second one is eight points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He got nine points of damage?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: And he's going to move slightly over here, basically getting you up against the wall. He's situating himself now where since you moved to the side; he's putting you in this pincer formation between the wall and himself. That ends his turn. You're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That hits, okay. Second attack is going to be a 19?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Next attack is going to be a 12, no, 13.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That misses.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. So the roundhouse kick (whoosh) swings around, completely unexpected as you pull to the side. (smack) Hits you right square in the side of the face and jaw. That is going to be seven points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And as you're reeling from that, he brings the leg back, jumps in the air and does a secondary jump, side-swiping kick to the other side of your head, as you're sent to the side. (whack, whack) Second one doing eight points of damage. (whack, whack)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. All right.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You take ten points of damage. Right to the chin from underneath. For the bonus action, Kern then comes back, and you're used to an unleashed flurry. Kern, however, comes back into a fully defensive position, righting his hands and leg in a backwards stance, and is just looking at you, ready for you to come. He's using his action to go into a dodge. It's now your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Now Kern, having landed on his feet, rushes forward towards you, and first using his bonus action, is going to go ahead and reach down for a very deep uppercut. It looks like he's coming in for a left, and sweeps down to try and take you from underneath, breaking through your defense. That is going to be a 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The next one, however, is eight points of damage. Swings up and actually haymakers you to the side with the hand still holding the chunk of your lip in it (slamming impact) across the side of your face, and is going to attempt to do a backwards leaping flip-kick. However, because Kern is not as practiced at this and is over-extending, you see it telegraphed far in advance, and you duck out of the way, and it (whoosh) whiffs past. Kern loses his balance for a minute and in an embarrassing moment almost falls over from his own strike. Manages to catch himself and is like, "Nobody saw that, right?" That ends Kern's turn. You're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wait, that was just my one attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's right. You still have-- No, you used your action to break the grapple. So you're still grappled, so you can't move. So this is his turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I broke the grapple!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You did break the grapple, so yeah, you could still move if you wanted to.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, I'll stay there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That is a 23.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 23. And a 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) Misses.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, grabs the lip in one hand, reaches up and then (slam) uppercuts you from underneath for the first hit. That is seven points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: One of the clerics looks up for a second. "Hold it there for a moment." And reaches out and administers a 5th-level Cure Wounds. He's making a medicine check to see how-- well, that's a 16. You go ahead and heal-- that's shitty rolls-- ten. I should've just rolled that one there. You heal 22 hit points, but you feel your lip fuse, and while it might leave a very small scar, it is in one piece. You might want to be careful about spitting or anything too heavy with it for the next few days for fear of jettisoning it from the front of your face accidentally.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The clerics now come climbing down to the center of the Crucible to retrieve the unconscious and bleeding-out body of Kern, administering Heals immediately to make sure he doesn't actually slip away from everyone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I run over and grab my lip and pick it up and go, just between you and me, is there anything we can do with this? (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: At three to one odds, that sack of gold you get back contains 5,452 gold pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys, by the way, right now are currently brought to a small holding facility not far from the Crucible, where you've been set down on a bench, both manacled and left while the one Bastion that originally found you is currently keeping watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Outside?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Out of vision of you guys. You don't see; they're just missing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Grog, before we go deal with our friends, if I may: That man fought a brave fight. I feel you should go make amends with him, it was a respectable battle. You won't feel good about yourself unless you do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At which point, the bookie hands a sack of gold right into your hand. "Well fought, new champion!" And hands it to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fuck that shit. (laughs) I was about to, but chaotic neutral, sorry! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Never mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I was ready.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And that, kids, is chaotic neutral.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He looks over at you, (groans) and you see for a minute he had forgotten about that in this exchange of honor, and some of the pain resurges, and (moans) starts whimpering, (coughs up spit) spits off to the side this giant bloody loogie that slaps on one of the sides of the cleric who's like, "Ugh!" Starts wiping it from the outside of his tunic. Looks back at you. "Greyskull. So be it. Ow." And hobbles away through a series of painful whimpers and whines. Percy, the bookie collects you. You bet 500 at a three to one; that is four times return. You make 2,000 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes! Strength and honor!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: However, there is no strength left. It's barely there. He takes his hand back and it hangs loosely to one side, and he looks at you and says, "I'm proud to have finally found my match. When I recover, I'm going to find you, and I'm going to want that championship back!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: When the time comes for a rematch, I'll be here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good. Where can I find you, Grog?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (quietly) Oh shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, yeah. Just tell him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: If I'm not here at the Crucible, because I don't think you need an audience for glory, you can always find me in Emon at Castle Greyskull.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Greyskull Keep!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Greyskull Keep!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a real place.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Good luck with your dick, by the way. Sorry about that.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The bookie gives you your coin purse of 20 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (shushing noises) There are maybe two men looking like late teens to early twenties who saw the fight and are currently like wide doe-eyed, seeing a hero before them.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I walk over to one of them and I pull out a gold coin and I say, how would you like to make a gold coin, son?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wait!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "From you, Grog the Victorious? Of course! What can I do?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I reach into the Bag of Holding. I take out the second-place medal, the participant medal, and I go, make sure Kern gets this. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He reads it. (chuckles) "Yes. Yes, indeed. Of course." And he runs off.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You sick fucks! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I mean, come on. You came into a bar looking for young boys. What was I supposed to think?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're together now. We're not arrested anymore. You don't have to keep doing this. All right! To Grog the Victorious!

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: To Grog!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Tiberius, you get your coin purse of 2,448.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I walk over to one of them and I pull out a gold coin and I say, how would you like to make a gold coin, son?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wait!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "From you, Grog the Victorious? Of course! What can I do?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I reach into the Bag of Holding. I take out the second-place medal, the participant medal, and I go, make sure Kern gets this. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He reads it. (chuckles) "Yes. Yes, indeed. Of course." And he runs off.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You sick fucks! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I mean, come on. You came into a bar looking for young boys. What was I supposed to think?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're together now. We're not arrested anymore. You don't have to keep doing this. All right! To Grog the Victorious!

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: To Grog!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The bookie gives you your coin purse of 20 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You guys, this really makes gambling so much fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't make a habit of it.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I was going to say, as you guys celebrate with a joyous drink of ale here at the Bellows' Respite Inn to celebrate the victory of not Philip, but Grog the Victorious in the Cauldron here in the Braving Grounds of Vasselheim in the northwestern region of the countryside, we will go and take a quick restroom break, return here in about five minutes. See you guys here shortly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're together now. We're not arrested anymore. You don't have to keep doing this. All right! To Grog the Victorious!

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: To Grog!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The bookie gives you your coin purse of 20 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You guys, this really makes gambling so much fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't make a habit of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Tiberius, you get your coin purse of 2,448.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Great, wonderful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: But you owe--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: No, she's paying for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She's got 20 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: She had other gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I bought a nice meal for you the other night.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's true. She bought a couple of nice meals for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit! That's right. I'll pay you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I lost 100 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What was it? 24?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 2,448 was the number.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So I guess I broke even.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You still netted a profit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you want to take the 500 from that, (mutters quietly) minus 500... 1,948.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That fight was amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was fantastic.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No. Actually, from where you guys are currently, across the Ozmit Sea is Emon, which is the port city in Tal'Dorei, and then Kraghammer is maybe two days' travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How about no?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: In great details of the internet. Just saying. I went there. It was already there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I was caught up in a moment of passion! I'm sorry!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Too late!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She would do anything for you, Grog!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Anything for you and for the 600 gold that that I put on your fight.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it was for the money!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Listen, I was right there. There was no other way around that situation.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You did what you had to.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Listen. I would show any part of me for, like, two gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Here's two gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Here, take a look at my third nipple!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: On your body?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It can be wherever I put it, really. It's magical.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: It's a piece of gum!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have no idea what's going on.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're all together. That's what's going on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I love this tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What is closer to Kraghammer? Emon? Or where we're from?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's halfway. I think Kraghammer is in between us and Emon.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: By now, I'd say it's probably 1 00 in the afternoon, since that fight's happened.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it was for the money!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Listen, I was right there. There was no other way around that situation.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You did what you had to.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Listen. I would show any part of me for, like, two gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Here's two gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Here, take a look at my third nipple!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: On your body?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It can be wherever I put it, really. It's magical.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: It's a piece of gum!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have no idea what's going on.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're all together. That's what's going on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I love this tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What is closer to Kraghammer? Emon? Or where we're from?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's halfway. I think Kraghammer is in between us and Emon.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. Actually, from where you guys are currently, across the Ozmit Sea is Emon, which is the port city in Tal'Dorei, and then Kraghammer is maybe two days' travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So we gotta stop into Emon first.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's fine. Are we all ready to go?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 14, okay. It takes you roughly 30 minutes or so. Thankfully, this region itself contains... honestly, most of the smithing-based materials and/or workshops they required. The difficult part is finding black powder. It is not refined in this region. It's usually considered almost a byproduct of some of the mining and smelting, and it's very rare to find. Eventually, you find one smithy that gathers it. You're not entirely certain why. And after you give people a couple of examples of the smell of it and what to look for, people send you in this one direction. And you're sent to this one small hovel that is attached to the very base of the mountain that overlooks the entirety of the city, on the far northwestern edge of the Braving Grounds. You come to the door, and it's a very simple, slovenly-kept house. The wood itself is a little warped from the weather; it hasn't kept well, and there's a musty smell of mildew from the outside of the building. But you see a slight glow between the cracks. Apparently, there is some sort of interior light.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Would you like me to go with you, Grog, as your manager?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I would actually like both the twins to go with.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Grog, would you like any young boys to accompany you? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You can give them a medal!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I went back into the bar because I went to take a piss outside.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, where're we going?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What is going on?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You guys are recovering from your drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hold on a second.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I've had plenty of water.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're already been arrested today.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm fine! (slurred) Scanlan has been ordering me water for the whole time has been cleared.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's right! I have already been arrested today, but I did get better.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so you guys are going with Percy to procure materials for forging new ammunition?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, go ahead and make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What happened to my dice? Okay. 14.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "A hundred pounds of iron ingots. I could sell it to you. Let's set the price at about 400 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Please."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No. Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Please. All right. What ore are you interested in?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just some iron ore. Actually, I have a little invention which sends projectiles at great speed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That sounds fascinating. I have ore for sale. Raw ore-- would you like ingots? I prepare ingots. They're a little more expensive, but they're proper and ready for any sort of use as blacksmithing materials."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Ingots would be preferable, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right, how many are you looking for? Five, ten, 20?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What could you give me for approximately a thousand gold's worth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He gives you an arched eyebrow in surprise. "I mean, I only have about 200 pounds of iron ingots at the ready."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I certainly can't carry that much with me right now. Let's say about a hundred pounds' worth?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: And the other one gets up to his side and stands there, arms crossed, looking down upon you from three large stone steps up with this look of, "I'm looking forward to throwing these guys out." About five minutes pass and the next one comes back and whispers in his ear... "Very well. The four of you may enter briefly. But this one's only invited to the center chamber. The rest of you must keep a distance. A distance."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, we are here to pay homage to Earthbreaker Groon with the latest champion of the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of the city? The city claims no champion!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of the Crucible. You know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They glance at each other.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The Earthbreaker is a fan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (chuckles) "I highly doubt that."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Tell him it's Grog's--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're not there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Check your facts, buddy. He likes the cut of his jib, believe you me.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: At this point in time, his eyelids close, and they open again, and there's a softness that there wasn't before. The white light has faded, and you can now see beneath them these extremely piercing very, very light blue, almost white irises that surround his pupils. And from his old, weathered, wrinkled face from underneath his beard, you can see a smile slowly crawl across his cheeks. His hands release and rest to his side. He lifts his head slightly as if to look down at you from upon this dais, not with judgment, but with curiosity. His voice echoes and booms through this with even but a whisper from his throat, once again saying, "Your piety is accepted. Perhaps there may be a use for Kord of you and your compatriots in the future. Should this day arise, you will know, and you must hurry. Dark times are ahead. I can see the veils twisting. Many, many evil minds come to rend the peace apart. There will be a day I call for you, Grog the Victorious. Stay alive."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You are Grog the Victorious."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's right! (coughs) That's right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Once called Philip. Known as one of Vox Machina, the travelling band from Emon. Slayer of demons that once usurped Uriel himself. I know of your travels. I know of your deeds. What else do you ask of the Earthbreaker?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't really ask. I only mean to say that Vox Machina is at your disposal, if you would ever need someone outside of the amazing Braving Grounds, Vasselheim. We're really amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: We know where they're at, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: We don't know where they're at?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: If you would just keep us in mind.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. So, as you've had this done over the past few days as you were awaiting the return of the other team who went off against a white dragon, apparently, you've had the time to do so, you just haven't quite revealed it to the rest of your allies yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, are you saying that someone out there in the world should do the elven symbol for VM and we should all get literally tattooed?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, that's ridiculous. But over here, I have a mathematical shape. I don't know if you know arithmetic, but I learned arithmetic when I was studying. I learned knife-work, I learned elven poetry, and I learned arithmetic and I had a knack for it. She didn't, but I did. And I would like to get a-- it was called an icosahedron-- right here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Right here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, are we all doing that together?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: In real life?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll go with you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is real life. But to start--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You guys, we got a little day-drunk. Let's go together. Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just me, the symbol for "vuh" and "mm" in Elven right there.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Oh. It is nearly a week to travel from Emon to Vasselheim in the airship, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, we just did this. It wasn't too long ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So it's okay, we popped out. Is the same guy there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Indeed. Gatekeeper Xanthus is there waiting for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long have we been gone?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hold on, Tiberius wants to say something.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: To explain the travel schematic, it would take us a few days to come back on the airship to this particular area if want to go back to Kraghammer. It would be much more efficient if we teleport, as we have, and take a transport to Kraghammer, which is only north of here, as well, which will only probably take a day, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Five days.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, did we leave Trinket?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Trinket is with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Airship. From here.

payload.raw_text: As you guys leave the Lyceum, you enter the main courtyard that leads into it. You are currently within the Cloudtop District of Emon, a clear blue sky dotted with clouds ahead of you. You gather it's probably around 2:00 or 3:00 in the afternoon. No time has passed, since the travel was instantaneous, thanks to teleportation.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: You know what I think? I think you guys hate each other, actually.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Be on your way. Goodbye. Please, leave now. Go."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Don't worry, I shat in the hallway.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: What time of day is it?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you guys leave the Lyceum, you enter the main courtyard that leads into it. You are currently within the Cloudtop District of Emon, a clear blue sky dotted with clouds ahead of you. You gather it's probably around 2:00 or 3:00 in the afternoon. No time has passed, since the travel was instantaneous, thanks to teleportation.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have a bathroom around here?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, Scanlan, hold it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Down the hall to the left."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. I might make it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax fills out the entire thing, except for the final signature, and says, Scanlan, could you put the final signature on this?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Notarize it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tiberius is helping.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh boy. Buttoning up my fly. Final signature? I'll just sign my name.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That works, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you kindly. You are free to go."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, it's been a pleasure."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: A real pleasure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very much so, thank you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you so much."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay, here we go. We'll go out this way.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You know what I think? I think you guys hate each other, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Be on your way. Goodbye. Please, leave now. Go."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Emon is so beautiful--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't worry, I shat in the hallway.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What time of day is it?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Cordell is looking over the edge as you approach and he gives you a wave. "Hello." And you can see Shayne runs up next to him off the top. She has her crossbow at the ready. She goes, "Oh! Finally, you've arrived. I'm so glad you could make it. Yes, this has been here for about two weeks now."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I need to water the garden. It's probably half dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: No, we have servants that do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To the keep!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To the keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You guys make your way through the Cloudtop District to the southern edge of Emon, to the lower slums outside of the city, along the southern wall to Greyskull Keep. The visual is welcoming. It looks standing, clean, and in the distance you can see a figure walking across the top of the wall, keeping watch. As you get closer, you can see it is the image of Cordell, the half-orc individual that you guys hired along with Shayne. As you approach the front gate, you see what looks like a tent: a large, thick burlap or linen, brown tent is set up right outside of you guys'--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That wasn't there before--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What? What is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Cordell. Cordell! What is this tent?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you throw open the tent, at that moment, you see another person is pushing out of the tent. And you both have this awkward moment where you both (awkward noises) and he pushes off of you instinctively and rights himself. The tent half collapses behind him and he catches himself and stands there at the ready. You see before you what looks to be a young man, probably in his late teens, 19 to 20 years old. Haphazardly dressed in scale mail armor, a longsword, crossbow, and handaxe, and buckler all dangling from his belts, weighing him down slightly. His dark brown eyes peer from beneath his short, tousled ash-blond hair. And a nervous yet excited smile flashes across his face. "Oh! You've returned! Ah. You! You, Percival. Percival, the great one who streaks fire from his tube. And the great druid princess. Keyleth, right? And you. You, the dragonborn who controls the elements himself. Tiberius of the Stormwind family, yes. Yes, I've heard of you. Scanlan. Scanlan I've heard your tales across the land. Your womanizing does not reach ears too far from the city."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That wasn't there before--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What? What is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Cordell. Cordell! What is this tent?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cordell is looking over the edge as you approach and he gives you a wave. "Hello." And you can see Shayne runs up next to him off the top. She has her crossbow at the ready. She goes, "Oh! Finally, you've arrived. I'm so glad you could make it. Yes, this has been here for about two weeks now."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is someone inside of it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At which point, you hear sudden rustling from inside of the tent and a face pops out, briefly. You can see what looks like humanoid features, and suddenly darts back inside. And you hear more rustling. It sounds like pots and metals clinking around.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I immediately go over there and throw open the--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I thought you were heroes. I waited two weeks to meet you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I've heard of your work for quite some time. Show me your ways."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Illithid, all right? Beholders. Large white dragons. I know it's cool to see her flash her boobs and this one sing a song. It's all excitement and craziness and all the rock and roll that the kids are talking about. But listen to me. It's dangerous. You could die any given day. So could I, so could she, so could him. Maybe not him, but the rest of us, sure. Are you really interested in this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an intimidation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Tough love. That's called tough love. It's not bad. That's a ten plus-- can somebody pass me that shit over there?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Plus six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks at you for a second. His brow comes together, and you can see there's a quiver to his bottom lip. He goes--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Wait, wait. There. There's his heart breaking.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: At which point, you hear a voice ring out from across the courtyard of the keep. "Ah! You've all returned!" And you see across the way, Laina, your joyful housekeeper, comes rushing out, arms open to greet you all. You can see there's a little bit of dirt on the front of her apron, like she's been cutting the hedges or doing some sort of garden work. She rushes out and hugs you. "It's been so long. It's been far too long. It's so good to see all of you."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: And if he comes for your head?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He won't.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he won't?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll deal with that, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did you never see The Incredibles? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, Tiberius?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: You and Grog I respect and fear the most out of all of us. You are cunning.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What am I, chopped liver?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I could crush you in one blow, Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's leave it at that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Vex, I fear you and am slightly attracted to you, as well. Grog and Vax, you are the strongest and most noble and cunning--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You live because I let you live. Just needed to say that out loud.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: You believe what you believe. I'm just saying, when it comes to things, sometimes you really rush into stuff, and stop. Because you could just be chill sometimes. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: A little tweak. A little dragonborn tweak. "Oh! Sorry, that reminds me. Hold on." And she pulls out a little piece of paper. You recognize it: it's used in various arcane messaging. She pulls out a small quill and dabbles it and starts writing a quick message on it. Closes the lid (fire burst noise). It disappears.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: You believe what you believe. I'm just saying, when it comes to things, sometimes you really rush into stuff, and stop. Because you could just be chill sometimes. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At which point, you hear a voice ring out from across the courtyard of the keep. "Ah! You've all returned!" And you see across the way, Laina, your joyful housekeeper, comes rushing out, arms open to greet you all. You can see there's a little bit of dirt on the front of her apron, like she's been cutting the hedges or doing some sort of garden work. She rushes out and hugs you. "It's been so long. It's been far too long. It's so good to see all of you."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: As I see her, I turn to greet, but I want to give Vax a little pinch on the butt. A little friendly pinch.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: A few more moments pass and you gather that this is a slow-moving, but a consistent moving, renovation of the temple of Sarenrae, based on the limited number of people that she has at her disposal to help with the process. But it seems to be going well.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She should fire that person, but okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He steps and finds a chair at one of the nearby tables, clustered towards the center, where you guys have pushed your dining tables together. "Now, while you were gone, a venture has been put into motion. In celebration of the agreed-upon construction of a grand bridge across the Shearing Channel to the eastern continent of Wynandia, a dinner is being held here within a week. This venture is helmed by one Lord and Lady Briarwood of Whitestone, as the bridge would be set at the edge of the Alabaster Sierras. They are probably en route as we speak. The bridge shall open trade routes with the kingdom of Wildmount across the way. I'm here because few things escape my eye. Percival, when last we were company, you had shifted so very uncomfortably at the mention of these two. May I ask why?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (groans) Is it bad news? We're not in the mood for bad news.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm all right. It's not bad news, it's just something I wanted to run by the rest of you."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Please feel free to speak.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I have my reservations, as well. My network has been watching their acquisition of Whitestone since the de Rolo family fell to disease, and there are far more questions than answers. I've sent operatives to inquire, but each has sent me a letter of resignation instead of choosing to stay within Whitestone under my employ. Now, our sovereign requested that I extend an invitation to this coming dinner-- per your request to him, Percy-- to you, Vox Machina. I am here to ask if you will attend, and if so, if I can trust you to keep an eye while I do my work."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Had I? I hadn't noticed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I did."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have had dealings with them before. I find them... untrustworthy.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I have my reservations, as well. My network has been watching their acquisition of Whitestone since the de Rolo family fell to disease, and there are far more questions than answers. I've sent operatives to inquire, but each has sent me a letter of resignation instead of choosing to stay within Whitestone under my employ. Now, our sovereign requested that I extend an invitation to this coming dinner-- per your request to him, Percy-- to you, Vox Machina. I am here to ask if you will attend, and if so, if I can trust you to keep an eye while I do my work."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Had I? I hadn't noticed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I did."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have had dealings with them before. I find them... untrustworthy.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Very good. I will contact you in the next few days and shall discuss further. Until then, enjoy the rest of your meal, and thank you." He sits up from his seat.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Had I? I hadn't noticed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I did."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have had dealings with them before. I find them... untrustworthy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I have my reservations, as well. My network has been watching their acquisition of Whitestone since the de Rolo family fell to disease, and there are far more questions than answers. I've sent operatives to inquire, but each has sent me a letter of resignation instead of choosing to stay within Whitestone under my employ. Now, our sovereign requested that I extend an invitation to this coming dinner-- per your request to him, Percy-- to you, Vox Machina. I am here to ask if you will attend, and if so, if I can trust you to keep an eye while I do my work."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What say you, Percy?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's Percy's call.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) Yes, we will attend.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "They are expected to arrive in the next five to seven days. The dinner is set for seven to eight days from now."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What say you, Percy?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's Percy's call.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) Yes, we will attend.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very good. I will contact you in the next few days and shall discuss further. Until then, enjoy the rest of your meal, and thank you." He sits up from his seat.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're having bread pudding for dessert.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm good, thank you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, when did you say this dinner was taking place?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "In about a week, they should be arriving."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me. I have a bit of work to do downstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we go to Kraghammer in that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Seven days in a week or in a week, like five days?
